steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is a transition connecting two sentences: the first says precise standards ensure scientific validity; the second says metrologists developed the SI system based on natural constants. The transition must show cause and effect.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In contrast,: The second sentence doesn't contradict the first — it follows logically from it. A contrast is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — Regardless,: Doesn't connect the cause (need for standards) to the effect (SI system). ✗
  • Choice C — In addition,: Suggests a side point, but the second sentence is the direct result of the first, not an aside. ✗
  • Choice D — For this reason,: Directly signals that the SI system was developed because precise standards are essential — a clean cause-to-effect link. ✓
That matches Choice D — For this reason,.
